data.title: International Financial Advisors Gets Regulatory Nod to Renew Treasury Shares Dealing Right
data.description:
International Financial Advisors (DFM: IFA) secured the Capital Markets Authority's approval to renew its right to deal in treasury shares.

The regulator's approval is valid for six months, the Dubai-based financial advisory services company said Thursday.

data.title: ANA Holdings and Singapore Airlines Expand Partnership with Revenue-Sharing Flights
data.description:
ANA Holdings and Singapore Airlines will launch revenue-sharing flights between Singapore and Japan starting in September, with joint fares available for sale in May.

Pending regulatory approval, they plan to extend their joint venture to key markets like Australia, India, Indonesia, and Malaysia, according to a joint statement on Thursday.

Since their 2020 joint venture agreement, ANA and SIA have significantly expanded codeshare options-ANA passengers now access 25 SIA destinations (up from 12), while SIA travelers can reach 34 ANA routes (up from 9), including 30 domestic Japanese flights.

data.title: Jarden Securities Upgrades Aristocrat Leisure to Overweight from Neutral, Price Target is AU$68
data.description: Aristocrat Leisure has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of AU$77.71, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: REG-Michelin: Publication of the preparatory documentation for the Annual Shareholders Meeting of May 16, 2025
data.description:
Clermont-Ferrand, April 18, 2025

COMPAGNIE GÉNÉRALE DES ÉTABLISSEMENTS MICHELIN

Publication of the preparatory documentation for the

Annual Shareholders Meeting of May 16, 2025

The Annual Meeting of Michelin Shareholders will be held on Friday, May 16, 2025 from 9 a.m. at the Zénith d'Auvergne, 24 rue de Sarliève, 63800 Cournon d'Auvergne, Puy-de-Dôme, France.

As Michelin shares are exclusively registered shares, all shareholders receive a notice of meeting including a voting form, the Meeting agenda, the resolutions submitted to their approval, and the main terms and conditions for participating and voting. This document, as well as the preliminary notice of meeting published in the French journal of record, Bulletin des Annonces Légales Obligatoires, within the legal deadlines, are available on the website michelin.com.

Shareholders can consult all the documentation referred to in Article R. 225-83 of the French Commercial Code on the above website or at the Company's headquarters. This information may also be requested from the Company.

The full event will also be broadcast live, in French and in English, on the Company's website, and the replay will remain available for a period of two years.

Investor calendar

  • April 24, 2025        Quarterly information for the three months ending March 31, 2025
  • May 16, 2025        Annual Shareholders Meeting
  • May 21, 2025        Ex-dividend date 
  • May 23, 2025        Dividend payment date
  • July 24, 2025        First-half 2025 results
  • October 22, 2025         Quarterly information for the nine months ending September 30, 2025

Contact details

  • Investor Relationsinvestor-relations@michelin.comGuillaume Jullienneguillaume.jullienne@michelin.comFlavien Huetflavien.huet@michelin.comBenjamin Marcusbenjamin.marcus@michelin.com
  • Media Relations+33 (0) 1 45 66 22 22groupe-michelin.service.de.presse@michelin.comIndividual Shareholders+33 (0) 4 73 32 23 05Muriel Combris-Battutmuriel.floc-hlay@michelin.com Elisabete Antuneselisabete.antunes@michelin.com

Attachment

  • 20250418_Michelin_PR_Publication of the preparatory documentation for the AGM

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Friday at 3 AM ET
data.description:
Dow Industrials Fall, Capping Another Tough Week on Wall Street

Nvidia stock slips amid trade tensions; Trump renews attacks on Powell.

U.S. Moves Ahead With Plan to Charge Fees on Chinese Ships

The U.S. trade representative plans to charge ships for each voyage, not for each U.S. port call.

Why World Leaders Are Pulling Their Punches in the Trade War

As countries negotiate with the Trump administration, they will have to decide whether to stick with that approach or try using retaliation as leverage.

Trump and Powell on Collision Course Without Easy Escape

The president wants an interest-rate cut, but his trade war has the Fed chair boxed in.

Private Equity World Engulfed by Perfect Storm

Tariff-related market turmoil dashes investors' hopes for payouts as dealmaking grinds to a near standstill.

Japan Inflation Speeds Up, Backing Case for Rate Hikes

Japan's core consumer inflation gained pace in March due to higher food prices, helping the Bank of Japan make a case for further interest-rate increases despite tariff concerns.

Bonds struggle since 'liberation day' tariffs, but Fed is in no rush to cut rates

The U.S. bond market has been in rough patch since President Donald Trump announced "Liberation Day" tariffs.

Rare Earths Show What Trump Gets Right, and Wrong, on Trade

China understands that there are more weapons in a trade war besides tariffs. The White House should consider what that means for U.S. trade policy.

Americans Worry Over Negative Impact of China Tariffs

Respondents in a recent Pew survey see trade as unbalanced, but are divided over the merit of increasing levies.

Tourists Are Avoiding the U.S. It's a Problem for the Oil Market.

Fewer visitors mean not only fewer room bookings for hotels, but lower oil prices for the U.S. benchmark, West Texas Intermediate crude.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Friday at 3 AM ET
data.description:
U.S. House Committee Urges Two U.S. Banks to Withdraw From CATL's Listing

A U.S. congressional committee has urged Bank of America and JPMorgan to pull out of working on the Chinese battery giant's planned listing in Hong Kong.

Bill Ackman Teases Prospect of Uber, Hertz Partnership

Billionaire investor discloses nearly 20% stake in Hertz, lays out path to greater profitability

Netflix, Unscathed by Tariffs, Posts Record Profit

The streamer forecasts stronger revenue growth in the second quarter.

Apple Loses Ground in China's Smartphone Market as Local Rivals Gain

Apple lost its top spot in China's smartphone market, dethroned by local rival Xiaomi as Beijing's consumption-boosting subsidies help buoy demand for cheaper products.

The Week Google and Meta Faced an Antitrust Reckoning

The two titans of digital advertising are facing unprecedented legal threats over tactics they used to reach dominance.

It looks like Intel may have to go it alone. Can CEO Lip-Bu Tan fix the company solo?

Another rumored partner has dismissed the prospect of an arrangement with Intel, suggesting the company won't get outside help with its turnaround.

The Chip Industry Has Too Many Eggs in the AI Basket

TSMC struck an upbeat note after posting strong earnings, but tariffs and the trade war loom large.

L'Oreal Sales Rise as Europe Growth Offsets U.S. Weakness

L'Oréal Chief Executive Nicolas Hieronimus told analysts the beauty market is now more likely to grow this year at the lower end of the 4% to 4.5% range he predicted in February.

Judge Rules Google Operates Illegal Ad Monopoly

The tech giant faces multiple legal threats related to how it wields market power.

Hermès Is Hiking Prices. Other Luxury Companies Will Follow.

The luxury company will increase prices starting May 1.

data.title: Japan wholesale rice prices fall in March after stockpile release
data.description:
Japan's wholesale rice prices fell for the first time in eight months in March, partly helped by the government's release of the staple grain from its emergency stockpile, the agriculture ministry said on Friday.

Average prices fell 2% from February to 25,876 yen ($181.79) per 60 kilogrammes.

The government released 210,000 metric tons of rice to wholesalers from its emergency stock last month as part of Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba's attempt to ease consumers' pain from soaring food prices.

Supermarket prices, however, climbed for the 14th straight week in the seven days to April 6, separate data showed this week. The government has promised to continue releasing from its stockpile every month through July.

($1 = 142.3400 yen)

data.title: US Rubio: We need to determine whether it's possible to end the war in Ukraine
data.description:
• We need to determine whether it's possible to end the war in Ukraine.
  • Talking about a matter of days to make that clear.
  • If not possible, the President will possibly say "we're done".
  • We need to determine quickly whether it's doable.
  • If not possible, we have other priorities.
  • Had positive meetings on Thursday.
  • Hope to hear back from Ukrainians soon.

data.title: Nikkei Rises 1.0%, Led by Pharma, Shipping StocksMarket Talk
data.description: Japanese stocks ended higher as fears about U.S. trade policy receded for now. Pharmaceutical and shipping stocks led gains. Chugai Pharmaceutical jumped 18% after Eli Lilly's weight-loss drug orforglipron, which was discovered by Chugai, showed positive study results. Kawasaki Kisen gained 3.7% and Mitsui O.S.K. Lines climbed 2.8% as the U.S. moved ahead with its plan to charge fees on Chinese ships. The Nikkei Stock Average rose 1.0% to 34730.28. The 10-year Japanese government bond yield fell 2 basis points to 1.285%. Investors continued to focus on U.S. tariffs and their economic implications. USD/JPY was at 142.35, compared with 142.45 as of Thursday 5 p.m. ET. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

data.title: Indonesia, U.S. Set 60-Day Deadline to Resolve Tariff Dispute
data.description:
Indonesia and the U.S. have agreed to a 60-day deadline to conclude negotiations over a 32% tariff imposed on Indonesian imports to the U.S., according to Coordinating Minister for Economic Affairs Airlangga Hartarto.

Key areas under discussion include investment, trade, critical minerals, and supply chain resilience.

As part of the effort to ease U.S. tariff pressure, Indonesia said on Friday it would increase oil and gas imports from the U.S. and continue purchasing American agricultural products such as wheat, soybeans, and soybean milk.

Hartarto emphasized that President Prabowo Subianto had sent a delegation to seek compromise on the tariff issue.

The team has met with top U.S. officials, including Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick, with a meeting scheduled next week with Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.

data.title: Nestlé India Board to Consider Final Dividend for Fiscal 2025
data.description: Nestlé India said its board of directors will meet on April 24 to consider the declaration of a final dividend for fiscal 2025 ended March 31, according to a filing with the Indian bourses on Thursday.

data.title: Beijing Shougang Returns to Profit in Q1; Shares Up 4%
data.description:
Beijing Shougang returned to profit in the first quarter after recording a net profit attributable to shareholders of 327.9 million yuan or 0.0423 yuan, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Friday.

The attributable loss a year earlier was 24.3 million yuan or 0.0031 yuan.

The Chinese steel manufacturer's operating income dropped 10% to 26.5 billion yuan from 29.5 billion yuan in the previous year, the filing said.

The company's shares jumped less than 4% in recent trade.

data.title: Takaful Emarat - Insurance Denies Affiliation with Takaful Emarat Insurance Services
data.description:
Takaful Emarat - Insurance (DFM:TAKAFUL-EM) said it has no legal or commercial affiliation with Takaful Emarat Insurance Services, a company suspended by the Saudi Insurance Authority from providing insurance brokerage activities on Wednesday.

The Dubai-based insurer issued the clarification on Thursday to avoid any confusion due to the similarity in the companies' names, according to a same-day release.

data.title: Nikkei Gains as Investors Await Updates on US-Japan Trade Talks
data.description:
Japanese shares edged up on Friday, with investors largely holding back ahead of developments in Japan-US tariff talks, which officials described as making "big progress."

The Nikkei 225 climbed 1.03%, or 352.68 points, to close at 34,730.28.

US President Donald Trump touted "big progress" in trade talks with Japan on Wednesday, a meeting Tokyo had expected to be low-profile.

Japan's Finance Minister Katsunobu Kato expressed concern, saying Trump's trade measures could jeopardize Japan's recovery.

In economic news, Japan's inflation eased slightly to 3.6% in March from 3.7% in February, while core CPI rose to 3.2%, up from 3.0%. Food prices surged 6.2%, and energy costs increased 6.6%.

The data bolstered the Bank of Japan's tightening stance, as inflation remains well above the 2% target.

On the corporate front, First Brothers reported a 7.1% increase in attributable profit to 68 million yen for the three months ended Feb. 28, driven by stronger real estate sales.

Net sales grew 20% to 2.34 billion yen. For the full year, it expects attributable profit to dip 3.3%, with net sales rising 6.4%. A year-end dividend of 35.00 yen per share is planned.

MarkLines formed a joint venture with Huaqin Technology to develop software for intelligent vehicles, focusing on the Japanese market.

MISUMI Group is set to acquire Fictiv Inc. for $350 million to enhance its digital services. Fictiv will operate as a subsidiary.

data.title: KRKA, TOVARNA ZDRAVIL D.D. - Acquisition of treasury shares
data.description:
POLISH FINANCIAL SUPERVISION AUTHORITY

UNI - EN REPORT No 21 / 2025

Date of issue: 2025-04-18

Short name of the issuer

KRKA

Subject

Acquisition of treasury shares

Official market - legal basis

Unofficial market - legal basis

Contents of the report:

Based on the provisions of the Rules of the Ljubljana Stock Exchange, d. d., Ljubljana and Article

2 of the Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2016/1052 Krka, d. d., Novo mesto announces:

With a reference to the public notice from 20 July 2023, Krka is publicly disclosing information on

transactions relating to buy-back programme in the period from 11 April 2025 up to and including 17

April 2025. Krka acquired 9,417 treasury shares in the total value of EUR 1,607,783 on the Ljubljana

Stock Exchange as follows:

- on 11 April 2025: 2,031 shares at a VWAP of EUR 169.75,

- on 14 April 2025: 2,044 shares at a VWAP of EUR 169.92,

- on 15 April 2025: 1,970 shares at a VWAP of EUR 170.12,

- on 16 April 2025: 1,911 shares at a VWAP of EUR 171.73 and

- on 17 April 2025: 1,461 shares at a VWAP of EUR 172.76.

Price is calculated as volume weighted average price (VWAP).

Ilirika d.d., Intercapital d.d. and NLB d.d. were brokers in these transactions. Detailed report on

acquisition of treasury shares attached.

The total number of treasury shares prior to these transactions was 2,223,970 representing 6.782%

of all shares issued.

The total number of treasury shares after these transactions is 2,233,387 representing 6.810% of

all shares issued.

The notice will be published on the Company's website (www.krka.biz) from 18 April 2025 onwards,

for a minimum period of 5 years.

Annexes

File Description

20250418_084845_0578103246_Detailed_report_on_acquisition_of_treasury_shares.pdf
